paper_focus.title: Duplex microstructure enhanced mechanical property and underlined mechanism in Fe35MnxAl0.1C low-density steel
paper_focus.research_objective: To elucidate the dislocation patterns and work hardening mechanisms in ferrite-based duplex Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel, particularly the role of edge dislocations and dynamic slip band refinement, in order to understand the enhanced ductility of the 8Al steel.
material_systems: Fe-35Mn-8Al-0.1C (8Al) – ferrite-based duplex steel with DO3 ordered domains
Fe-35Mn-10Al-0.1C (10Al) – nearly fully ferritic steel with B2/DO3 ordered phases
Key phases: austenite (γ), ferrite (α), DO3 ordered domains, B2 ordered phases
main_process_variables: Hot forging to φ 16.5 mm rods
Solution treatment at 1050 °C for 2 h followed by oil quenching
Tensile deformation at room temperature (from 0 to ~30% engineering strain)
